query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "interleavethinker: reinforcing agentic interleaved generation" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it addresses the same core mechanism of "agentic interleaved generation" in a specific domain (terminal/Docker environments) and provides empirical evidence on training models with generated agent trajectories, which is the direct methodological follow-up to the "InterleaveThinker" work mentioned in the user's query.
